Pierre-Auguste Renoir

32 works in the gallery.
Click any image to enlarge. Use ← → or close with Esc.
Romaine Lacaux
by Pierre-Auguste Renoir
France, 19th century · 1864 · oil on fabric
Woman at the Piano
by Pierre-Auguste Renoir
France · Impressionism · 1875–1876 · Oil on canvas
Lunch at the Restaurant Fournaise (The Rowers' Lunch)
by Pierre-Auguste Renoir
France · Impressionism · 1875 · Oil on canvas
Dance at Le Moulin de la Galette
by Pierre-Auguste Renoir
Pierre-Auguste Renoir · 1876 · Musée d'Orsay
A Sunday afternoon at the Montmartre dance hall, sunlight filtering through acacia leaves onto the dancers in dapples Renoir invented a new vocabulary of color to paint. His friends posed for it over several months.
Alfred Sisley
by Pierre-Auguste Renoir
France · Impressionism · 1876 · Oil on canvas
The Laundress
by Pierre-Auguste Renoir
France · Impressionism · 1877–1879 · Oil on canvas
Acrobats at the Cirque Fernando (Francisca and Angelina Wartenberg)
by Pierre-Auguste Renoir
France · Impressionism · 1879 · Oil on canvas
Seascape
by Pierre-Auguste Renoir
France · Impressionism · 1879 · Oil on canvas
Near the Lake
by Pierre-Auguste Renoir
France · Impressionism · 1879–1880 · Oil on canvas
Young Woman Sewing
by Pierre-Auguste Renoir
France · Impressionism · 1879 · Oil on canvas
Luncheon of the Boating Party
by Pierre-Auguste Renoir
Pierre-Auguste Renoir · 1881 · The Phillips Collection
Renoir's friends — among them his future wife Aline, caressing a little dog — laze on a restaurant terrace overlooking the Seine. It is impressionism at its most convivial: no protest, no weather, just a warm afternoon extended indefinitely.
